description: A structured workflow for incrementally improving module documentation, clarity, and organization

steps.description: Choose a module that needs documentation improvement or code clarification

steps.action: Clarify module purpose and domain concepts
steps.description: When uncertain about module purpose, domain concepts, or architectural intent:
subtasks: Read module code and related documentation
Analyze usage patterns in other modules
Identify unclear or ambiguous concepts
Request clarification from maintainer for domain-specific questions
Document architectural intent and design decisions
Verify understanding before proceeding with documentation
steps.output: Clear understanding of module purpose and domain concepts

steps.action: Provide module purpose explanation for review
steps.description: Before documenting, present module purpose analysis to maintainer:
subtasks: Summarize module's role in the system
Explain key components and their relationships
Describe usage patterns and dependencies
Identify any architectural questions or uncertainties
Request feedback and corrections before proceeding

steps.description: Read the entire module to understand its structure, purpose, and current documentation state
steps.output: Complete understanding of module's current state

steps.description: Analyze the module's architecture, design patterns, and usage throughout the codebase to identify:
subtasks: Architectural strengths and weaknesses
Code complexity and potential simplifications
Performance considerations
Usage patterns in other modules
Design pattern consistency
Potential refactoring opportunities
Integration with other systems
Critical dependencies and their stability
Error handling and failure modes
Domain-specific patterns and conventions
Authoring patterns and customisation points
Complexity analysis: overly complex areas, simplification opportunities
Type signature review: can signatures be simplified?
Unnecessary functions/fields: identify redundant or unused components
API design review: consistency and usability assessment
steps.output: Comprehensive architectural analysis report with simplification recommendations

steps.description: Before documenting, present architectural findings to maintainer:
subtasks: Summarize architectural strengths and weaknesses
Highlight any potential issues or concerns
Identify architectural patterns and anti-patterns
Request feedback on architectural decisions
Get approval before proceeding with documentation

steps.description: Analyze current architecture and propose concrete improvements:
subtasks: Identify specific architectural issues
Propose concrete improvements for each issue
Evaluate improvement feasibility and impact
Prioritize improvements by benefit/cost ratio
Document proposed changes clearly
Request feedback on improvement proposals

steps.description: Systematically check all documentation for completeness and quality:
subtasks: Verify module header completeness (copyright, license, maintainer, description)
Check all exported types have haddock documentation
Check all exported functions have haddock documentation
Check all type classes have haddock documentation
Identify insufficient documentation (too brief, unclear, or unhelpful)
Identify unprofessional documentation (negative tone, poor phrasing)
Identify confusing documentation (ambiguous, misleading, or incorrect)
Check for inconsistent terminology or naming
Verify documentation follows established patterns and conventions
Identify missing cross-references to related modules/types
steps.output: Comprehensive documentation audit report with specific findings

steps.description: Create a detailed list of potential improvements beyond documentation:
subtasks: Module header documentation enhancement
Type and class documentation improvements
Clarification of confusing or redundant comments
Explanation of complex constructs
Documentation of instance purposes
Simplification of overly complex code
Consistency improvements
Export list organization improvements
Cross-reference additions
steps.output: Todo list with prioritized improvement areas

steps.description: Maintain comprehensive records of cleanup progress:
subtasks: Add completed module to cleanup tracking list
Update remaining modules list
Record any architectural findings or decisions
Document any bugs found and fixes applied
Update overall progress statistics
Provide clear summary of what was accomplished
steps.output: Updated cleanup tracking with completed work and remaining tasks

steps.description: Show the user the identified improvement areas as a todo list for selection
steps.output: User selects specific area(s) to work on

steps.description: Implement the chosen documentation or code improvements
process: Analyse current documentation issues in detail
Discuss changes with user for review
Apply approved changes using British spelling conventions

steps.description: Check that improvements are correct and consistent with project style

steps.action: Repeat or move to next module
steps.description: Either continue with remaining improvements for this module or select next module
After completing module cleanup, verify the project still compiles without errors
process: Fix any issues before marking module as complete

guiding_principles: Focus on clarity and maintainability
Preserve existing functionality
Follow project documentation conventions (including British spelling)
Prioritize high-impact improvements
Make incremental, verifiable changes
Maintain consistency with similar modules
Use positive, constructive documentation style
Provide concrete examples where helpful
Always propose changes in text for review before applying
Update todo lists immediately after each change
Proceed to next item automatically after applying changes
Check for downstream usage when adding new functions
Maintain consistent terminology and naming conventions
Perform thorough architectural analysis before making changes
Document architectural strengths and weaknesses explicitly
Only propose architectural changes after comprehensive analysis
Present all findings for review before implementation
Always perform comprehensive documentation audit before proposing changes
Check for missing, insufficient, confusing, or unprofessional documentation
Verify all exported items have appropriate haddock documentation
Ensure documentation follows established quality standards
Request clarification when uncertain about domain concepts or architectural intent
Never guess at module purpose - always verify understanding first
Document architectural decisions explicitly for future maintainers
Maintain comprehensive module cleanup tracking
Record completed modules and update remaining module list
Provide clear progress reporting and status updates
Create todo items for identified architectural improvements
Track architectural improvements separately from documentation tasks
Prioritize architectural improvements by impact and feasibility
Prefer haddock inline comments (-- ^) over @x@ style argument documentation
Use complete sentences in haddock comments
Avoid sales pitch language in documentation
Provide usage examples in documentation where helpful
Document edge cases and error conditions
Use British spelling consistently throughout
Add cross-references to related modules and types where appropriate
Include module header with copyright, license, and maintainer in every module

examples: Enhance module header with comprehensive overview
Add missing haddock comments for types and functions
Replace negative phrasing with positive explanations
Add missing property query functions for complete coverage
Ensure consistent naming patterns (e.g., roomIs*, thingIs*)
Replace inconsistent functions with unified patterns
Add functions proactively before they're needed

established_workflow_patterns.change_proposal: Always present proposed changes in text format for review before applying file updates
established_workflow_patterns.todo_management: Update todo lists immediately after completing each item
established_workflow_patterns.automatic_progression: Proceed to next todo item automatically after applying changes
established_workflow_patterns.downstream_check: When adding new functions, search for potential usage locations
consistency_rules: Use 'a thing'/'a room' not 'this thing'/'this room'
Use complete sentences in haddock comments
Explain what functions do, not how they work

header_structure: See also section with related modules
